page_content:
Restriction: Permission of ENGR-Materials Science & Engineering department.

Credit Only Granted for. ENMA684 or ENMA698I.

Formerly: ENMA698I.

ENMA685 Advanced Electrical and Optical Materials (3 Credits) Students become familiar with basic and state of the art knowledge of some technologically relevent topics in materials engineering and applied physics, including dielectric/ferroelectric materials, magnetic materials, superconductors, multiferroic materials and optical materials with an underlying emphasis on the thin film and device fabrication technology. Fundamental physical properties and descriptions of different materials and their applications are included. Discussion will include new developments in the fields.
